Operation 'searchLogs' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. While the current description mentions key features (SQL queries, time-range filtering, pagination), it lacks clarity about expected inputs (SearchRequest schema details) and outputs (SearchResponse structure). Added explicit mention of request/response schemas and clarified that this is a read-only operation with no side effects, improving AI agent interpretability for determining when to use this operation.

data.current_description: Execute SQL queries against log streams with time-range filtering and pagination.
data.summary_suggestion: Execute SQL queries against log streams
data.description_suggestion: Execute SQL queries against log streams with time-range filtering and pagination support. Accepts a SearchRequest containing the SQL query, time range, and pagination parameters. Returns a SearchResponse with matching log records, result count, and query execution metadata. Does not modify any data or state.
data.reasoning: While the current description mentions key features (SQL queries, time-range filtering, pagination), it lacks clarity about expected inputs (SearchRequest schema details) and outputs (SearchResponse structure). Added explicit mention of request/response schemas and clarified that this is a read-only operation with no side effects, improving AI agent interpretability for determining when to use this operation.

message: Operation 'createUser' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Current summary and description are too generic. Enhanced summary to include 'organization' context. Expanded description to specify expected inputs (CreateUserRequest fields), outputs (StatusMessage with user identifier), required permissions, and side effects (account becomes immediately active). This provides AI agents with clear understanding of prerequisites, inputs, outputs, and state changes needed to determine appropriate usage.

data.current_description: Create a new user account within an organization.
data.summary_suggestion: Create a new user account in organization
data.description_suggestion: Create a new user account within an organization. Accepts a CreateUserRequest containing user details such as username, email, password, and role assignment. Returns a StatusMessage confirming successful user creation with the new user's identifier. Requires organization-level permissions. The new user account is immediately active and can authenticate with provided credentials.
data.reasoning: Current summary and description are too generic. Enhanced summary to include 'organization' context. Expanded description to specify expected inputs (CreateUserRequest fields), outputs (StatusMessage with user identifier), required permissions, and side effects (account becomes immediately active). This provides AI agents with clear understanding of prerequisites, inputs, outputs, and state changes needed to determine appropriate usage.

message: Operation 'deleteStream' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_side_effects. While the summary is clear, the description lacks important context about irreversibility, scope of deletion (logs, metrics, traces), permission requirements, and return value. Added these details to help AI agents understand the destructive nature and consequences of this operation.

data.current_description: Remove a complete stream and all associated data.
data.description_suggestion: Permanently removes a stream and all associated data including logs, metrics, and traces. This operation is irreversible and will delete all historical data stored in the specified stream. Requires appropriate permissions. Returns a status confirmation upon successful deletion.
data.reasoning: While the summary is clear, the description lacks important context about irreversibility, scope of deletion (logs, metrics, traces), permission requirements, and return value. Added these details to help AI agents understand the destructive nature and consequences of this operation.

message: Operation 'deleteStreamDataByTimeRange' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_side_effects. Description is too vague about input requirements (start/end timestamps), operation behavior (asynchronous), and side effects (irreversible deletion). Added details about required parameters, async nature, job tracking mechanism, and irreversibility to improve AI agent understanding of how to use this operation.

data.current_summary: Delete stream data by time range
data.current_description: Remove stream data within a specific timeframe.
data.description_suggestion: Removes stream data records within a specified time range. Requires start and end timestamps to define the deletion window. This operation is asynchronous and returns a job ID for tracking deletion progress. Use the delete job status endpoint to monitor completion. Deleted data cannot be recovered.
data.reasoning: Description is too vague about input requirements (start/end timestamps), operation behavior (asynchronous), and side effects (irreversible deletion). Added details about required parameters, async nature, job tracking mechanism, and irreversibility to improve AI agent understanding of how to use this operation.

message: Operation 'deleteCacheResults' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Description lacks clarity on what 'timestamp threshold' means and how it's used. Missing details about required parameters, what gets deleted (cached results with timestamps <= threshold), and return value. Enhanced description provides specific parameter requirements and clearer semantics for AI agent interpretation.

data.current_description: Clear cached query results up to a specified timestamp threshold.
data.description_suggestion: Clears cached query results for a stream up to a specified timestamp threshold. Requires a timestamp parameter to define the cache invalidation boundary. All cached results with timestamps earlier than or equal to the threshold will be removed. Returns a status message confirming the cache deletion operation.
data.reasoning: Description lacks clarity on what 'timestamp threshold' means and how it's used. Missing details about required parameters, what gets deleted (cached results with timestamps <= threshold), and return value. Enhanced description provides specific parameter requirements and clearer semantics for AI agent interpretation.

message: Operation 'ingestLogsJson' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Summary is clear and action-oriented. Description explains the format and field limitation but lacks information about what happens to the data (persistence, availability), return value structure, and error handling. Added details about data persistence, search availability, response format, and failure reporting for better AI agent understanding.

data.current_description: Upload multiple log records in batch using standard JSON format. Records limited to 200 fields maximum.
data.description_suggestion: Uploads multiple log records in batch using standard JSON format. Each record is limited to 200 fields maximum. Records are persisted to the specified stream and become immediately available for search and analysis. Returns an ingestion response with status and record count. Failed records are reported in the response.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and action-oriented. Description explains the format and field limitation but lacks information about what happens to the data (persistence, availability), return value structure, and error handling. Added details about data persistence, search availability, response format, and failure reporting for better AI agent understanding.

message: Operation 'ingestMetricsJson' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Description is too vague about input structure (what fields are expected in metric records), data handling (where/how data is stored), and output format. Added specifics about expected metric object structure (timestamp, name, value, tags), persistence behavior, and response format to help AI agents understand how to construct requests and interpret responses.

data.current_description: Upload multiple metric records in batch with standard JSON format.
data.description_suggestion: Uploads multiple metric records in batch using standard JSON format. Metric records are persisted to the metrics stream and become immediately available for querying and visualization. Expected input is an array of metric objects containing timestamp, name, value, and optional tags. Returns an ingestion response with status, record count, and any processing errors.
data.reasoning: Description is too vague about input structure (what fields are expected in metric records), data handling (where/how data is stored), and output format. Added specifics about expected metric object structure (timestamp, name, value, tags), persistence behavior, and response format to help AI agents understand how to construct requests and interpret responses.
